_comment:
Hand-curated orientations for spots where the geometric algorithm cannot resolve the seaward bearing. Applied by pipeline/enrich.py between Algorithm 1 (orientation) and Algorithm 2 (swell window), so the orientation-derived fallback builds arcs against the correct value. Treated as authoritative — overrides both the algorithmic result and LLM verification. Match by exact spot name.

WHEN TO ADD AN ENTRY:
- Great Lakes spots (GSHHG L1 lumps the lakes with land — orientation returns null)
- Complex harbors, jetties, rivermouths where the coastline normal misrepresents the actual break geometry
- Barrier-island spots whose Nominatim geocode landed on the bay side and whose automatic 180° hemisphere flip didn't cover it
- Spots in Alaska / Guam / territories not covered by the region hemisphere table

FIELDS:
- orientation_deg (required, 0-359): the seaward-facing bearing. 0=N, 90=E, 180=S, 270=W. This is the direction the rider looks when facing the incoming swell.
- source (optional): short attribution — 'local knowledge', 'surf-forecast.com manual check', 'OSM way heading', etc.
- notes (optional): any disambiguation that matters (e.g. 'Pier points straight offshore', 'Break faces the reef pass rather than the beach').

Grand Haven South Pier.notes: Pier points straight west into the lake

Grand Haven State Park.notes: Beach adjacent to the pier; same facing

Frankfort Beach.notes: Point Betsie area; open lake due west

Whihala Beach.notes: At the south tip of the lake; open water due north

Peahi Jaws.notes: Pe'ahi bowl faces NNW winter swell; slight east-of-north facing

Vilano Beach.source: Florida east coast near St. Augustine
Vilano Beach.notes: Beach faces ENE; slight bend at Vilano inlet

IB Jetty.source: Imperial Beach, CA — south jetty at the border
IB Jetty.notes: Jetty points SW into the Pacific

Gooch's Beach.notes: Atlantic-facing beach with a SE bend toward Wells Bay
